title: pilosella auriculoides subsp. longiseta
intro: Pilosella auriculoides subsp. longiseta is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, known for its distinctive elongated flower stalks. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its purported anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Pilosella auriculoides subsp. longiseta is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
